{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 80, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Turn-of-nut match marks absent or not rotated on structural bolts", "No visible evidence that bolts were tensioned using turn-of-nut method per RCSC Specification", "Bolt pretension cannot be verified by visual inspection"], "actions": ["Reject affected bolts and require turn-of-nut installation with match marking per RCSC", "Re-torque/re-tension affected bolts and re-mark after proper rotation", "Have qualified inspector witness and document bolt pretensioning"], "rationale": "The RCSC Specification requires high-strength structural bolts installed by the turn-of-nut method to have clearly visible match marks showing the required rotation from snug-tight condition. Absent or unrotated match marks mean the bolt pretension cannot be verified, creating a structural integrity risk in a heavy-industrial application.", "references": ["RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts - Turn-of-Nut Installation Method", "RCSC Specification - Inspection"]}
